jdbc_connector_workflow.overview: This workflow guides you through creating a custom JDBC connector for any database using the IBM Watson Data Platform Connect SDK. The process involves creating a DSL configuration, generating connector code, customizing it, generating tests, and building the final connector.

prerequisites.requirement: Access to the target database for testing
JDBC driver information for the target database

phase.name: information_gathering
phase.title: Gather Database Information
phase.description: Collect all necessary information about the target database before starting the connector creation process.

required_information.item: Database name (e.g., db2, mysql, postgresql, oracle)
Default port number for the database
Whether database supports schemas, catalogs, or both

actions.action: Ask user for the database name they want to create a connector for
Determine if user has JDBC driver information or needs help finding it
Validate that we have all required information before proceeding

phase.name: dsl_creation
phase.title: Create DSL Configuration
phase.description: Create a database-specific DSL configuration file based on DSL-jdbc.json template. This file defines all connector properties, driver information, and customizations.

step.action: Create new DSL file named DSL-jdbc-{database}.json

fields.field: connector_description: Brief description of the connector

validation.check: Verify connector_label is user-friendly and descriptive
Ensure driver_class_name is correct for the database
Confirm jdbc_url template uses correct syntax
Validate driver_dependencies array is properly formatted

phase.name: label_confirmation
phase.title: Confirm Connector Label
phase.description: Ask the user to confirm the connector_label before proceeding, as this will be visible in the UI and should be user-friendly.

actions.action: Extract connector_label from the created DSL file
Use ask_followup_question to confirm with user
If user wants to change it, update the DSL file

phase.example_question: I've set the connector label to "{label}". This will be displayed in the Cloud Pak for Data UI. Is this label acceptable, or would you like to change it?

phase.name: code_generation
phase.title: Generate Connector Template Code
phase.description: Run the autoGenerateJavaJdbc Gradle task to generate the connector skeleton code from the DSL configuration.

step.action: Change to sdk-gen directory if not already there

step.note: The DSL file path is relative to sdk-gen directory

step.action: Wait for task completion and verify success

phase.name: dsl_relocation
phase.title: Move DSL to Connector Directory
phase.description: Move the DSL configuration file to the generated connector's resources directory for use in subsequent customization steps.

step.action: Create the target directory if it doesn't exist
Copy DSL file to connector directory
step.tool: execute_command with cp or use read_file + write_to_file

phase.name: connector_customization
phase.title: Customize Connector Code
phase.description: Run the customizeJdbcConnector task to apply DSL configuration to the generated connector code, updating driver class names, connection URLs, and properties.

what_it_does.update: Updates Connector.java with correct JDBC driver class name
Modifies getConnectionURL() method to build correct JDBC URL

phase.name: test_generation
phase.title: Generate Test Suite
phase.description: Generate comprehensive property validation tests for the connector using the generateJdbcTests task.

phase.name: code_formatting
phase.title: Apply Code Formatting
phase.description: Run spotlessApply to format all generated and customized code according to project standards.

phase.name: build
phase.title: Build Connector
phase.description: Build the connector without running tests to verify compilation and packaging.

phase.name: flight_app_generation
phase.title: Generate Flight App
phase.description: Automatically generate a Flight app project that includes the newly created connector. This creates a deployable application with the connector integrated.

step.note: This task automatically determines the connector name from the DSL file

success_indicators.indicator: Flight app directory created at subprojects/flight/
Connector dependency added to flight app build.gradle

step.action: Verify flight app can be built
step.note: This step is optional but recommended to verify the flight app is properly configured

what_it_does.action: Makes connector ready for Cloud Pak for Data integration

completion_criteria.criterion: DSL configuration file created and validated
DSL moved to connector resources directory
Flight app generated with connector integrated

next_steps_for_user.step: Review generated connector code in subprojects/jdbc_{database}/
Review generated flight app in subprojects/flight/
Deploy to Cloud Pak for Data (see deployment documentation)

error.action: Check Gradle output for specific error messages
Verify DSL file syntax is valid JSON
Ensure all required fields are filled in DSL
Check that working directory is correct (sdk-gen/)
Consult database documentation for JDBC driver details
Check Maven Central for driver coordinates
Check for compilation errors in generated code
Verify JDBC driver dependency is correct
